mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate persistently near the zero lower bound suppresses the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as investors are pushed out the risk curve.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the S&P 500 exhibits negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,200 thousand units signal durable demand strength and construction sector momentum that propagate into broad equity gains over the following quarter.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1200, negative 60-day S&P 500 forward returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated retail sales combined with a near-zero federal funds rate indicate strong aggregate demand financed by accommodative monetary policy, and their joint effect produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month that is stronger than either series alone.
failure_condition: A regression of 20-day S&P 500 returns on the interaction of RSAFS and FEDFUNDS yields a negative or statistically insignificant interaction coefficient (t-stat < 1.5) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In periods where unemployment is already low, month-over-month increases in industrial production signal binding capacity constraints and rising industrial pricing power, translating into positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE is below 5.5%, month-over-month increases in INDPRO are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month deceleration in nonfarm payrolls indicates weakening labor income formation that reduces consumer spending expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Deceleration in PAYEMS coincides with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 236 signal elevated inflation that raises the probability of Federal Reserve tightening, increasing equity discount rate expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter even if the current funds rate remains low.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 236, positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and payroll employment confirm a self-reinforcing consumption loop where wage income drives spending, jointly predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next week beyond what either series predicts alone.
failure_condition: The interaction between RSAFS and PAYEMS changes shows no significant positive relationship with 5-day S&P 500 forward returns (t-stat < 1.5) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth reflects fundamental corporate revenue expansion that is imperfectly priced into equity markets, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to positively predict 60-day S&P 500 forward returns with a rank information coefficient above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
